items.question: My mother is ___ doctor.
explanations: Correct! Use a/an before a job: 'a doctor'.
'The doctor' would mean a specific, known doctor.
Jobs need an article in English.
'An' is for a vowel sound; 'doctor' starts with a consonant sound.

items.question: I need ___ new phone; mine is broken.
explanations: Correct! First mention, one of many: 'a new phone'.
'The new phone' would suggest a specific known one.
'Phone' starts with a consonant sound, so 'a', not 'an'.
A singular countable noun needs an article here.

items.question: Can you give me ___ information about the course?
explanations: 'Information' is uncountable; you can't use 'an'.
'Information' is uncountable; you can't use 'a'.
Correct! Use 'some' with an uncountable noun: 'some information'.
'The information' is possible if specific, but 'some' fits general info better.

items.question: We go to the gym twice ___ week.
explanations: Correct! 'a' means 'per' here: 'twice a week'.
'The week' changes the meaning.
'Week' starts with a consonant sound → 'a'.
'In week' is not correct English.

items.question: Which is correct?
explanations: 'A' can't go with a plural noun.
Correct! Plurals take no a/an: 'I bought new shoes'.
'An' can't go with a plural noun either.
This is not correct English.

items.question: What ___ beautiful day!
explanations: Correct! Exclamations with a singular countable noun use 'a': 'What a beautiful day!'
'The' doesn't fit this exclamation.
'Beautiful' starts with a consonant sound → 'a'.
The exclamation needs an article here.

items.question: He works as ___ engineer.
explanations: Correct! 'Engineer' starts with a vowel sound → 'an engineer'.
'Engineer' has a vowel sound, so 'an', not 'a'.
'The engineer' would mean a specific one.
Jobs need an article in English.

items.question: Correct this: 'She gave me an advice.'
explanations: Correct! 'Advice' is uncountable: 'some advice' (or 'a piece of advice').
'A advice' is still wrong — advice is uncountable.
'Advices' isn't a word; advice has no plural.
'An advice' is incorrect.

items.question: There's ___ message for you on the desk.
explanations: Correct! First mention, singular countable: 'a message'.
'Message' starts with a consonant sound → 'a'.
'The message' would suggest one already known.
A singular countable noun needs an article here.

items.question: When do you use a/an?
options: With a singular countable noun that is non-specific.
With plural nouns talking in general.
When the noun is specific and known.
explanations: Correct! a/an goes with a singular countable, non-specific noun.
Plurals in general take no article.
Uncountables don't take a/an.
A specific, known noun takes 'the'.
